svg.title: Airbnb Data Scientist end-to-end pipeline — skills, data, and Sr DS ownership
svg.desc: Nine-stage vertical pipeline for Airbnb Data Scientists covering raw event ingestion through feature engineering, three core ML domains (search ranking, price prediction, trust and safety), experimentation, model training, serving, monitoring, and the feedback loop — with data schemas, skills required, and Senior DS ownership annotated at every stage.
svg.text: Stage 1 — Raw event sources (two sides of the marketplace)
Stage 2 — Stream processing and offline warehouse
Zipline feature store (Airbnb-built) — online (Redis) · offline (S3) · time-travel queries
point-in-time correct features for every listing, guest, and host
Sr DS owns: training-serving skew prevention — features at prediction time must match features used at training time exactly
Sr DS owns: no future leakage — booking outcome cannot appear as a feature; only signals known BEFORE the booking decision
Stage 4 — Three core ML domains Airbnb DS works across
Sr DS owns: metric selection per domain — NDCG for ranking, booking rate for pricing, PR-AUC for trust (not ROC)
Sr DS owns: reproducibility (code SHA + data snapshot + seed), regression gate (new model must not drop NDCG >0.5%)
Sr DS owns: slice analysis — does model regress on any country, price tier, or property type? Global NDCG can hide a segment failure.
Stage 7 — Real-time serving — 100ms total budget for search
Sr DS owns: graceful degradation — if DNN times out, fall back to LightGBM score. Never return empty results.
Sr DS owns: booking-delay correction — bookings take 2–14 days, so 2-week tests miss revenue signal. Run 4+ weeks for reliable booking rate estimates.
Impression log ⋈ booking outcome (Spark, nightly) → training labels → daily retrain
booking delay: labels arrive 2–14 days after impression → retrain uses rolling 90-day window
Sample tables an Airbnb DS queries every day
Click any node to explore that stage in depth
